latex: If you can trade three apples for two oranges, and you can trade three oranges for five lemons, how many apples would you need to trade for $100$ lemons?
notes: }  \textbf{Answer:} We can equate ratios to figure out how many oranges we need and then how many apples we need.$$\frac{3\text{ oranges}}{5\text{ lemons}}=\frac{a\text{ oranges}}{100\text{ lemons}}\qquad\Rightarrow a=3\cdot20=60\text{ oranges}$$$$\frac{3\text{ apples}}{2\text{ oranges}}=\frac{b\text{ apples}}{60\text{ oranges}}\qquad\Rightarrow b=3\cdot30=90\text{ apples}$$We need $\boxed{90}$ apples to trade for 100 lemons.       }
